sourceMovie.title: The Transporter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Transporter for the same reason it broke through in 2002: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Corey Yuen keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Fate of the Furious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Transporter clicked for you, The Fate of the Furious (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Transporter?
faq.answer: Try The Fate of the Furious, Transporter 2, Ronin—each shares DNA with The Transporter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Transporter?
faq.answer: The Transporter (2002) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Transporter part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Transporter Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
